title: BayonHub

screenshots.caption: Buyer discovery, filters, and listing cards.
Category browsing with premium seeded vehicles.
Seller trust, listing details, safety/contact surface.
Seller posting flow on a phone-sized viewport.

problem: Cambodian students in AI engineering, data science, cybersecurity, software engineering, and related tech majors need a focused place to find internships, freelance work, jobs, and portfolio-building opportunities.
context: BayonHub began as a Khmer-first classifieds marketplace prototype. That prototype proved I could build search, posting, trust, bilingual UI, and offline demo flows. The product has now pivoted into a tech-student opportunity hub, reusing the lessons from the marketplace build while replacing the domain model around students, employers, opportunities, applications, and student profiles.
constraints: Solo student project — no team or funding
Designing for Cambodian tech students and employers
Keeping old marketplace proof visible without pretending it is the current product direction
myRole: I built everything myself — frontend, backend, basic DevOps, and product decisions — as a learning exercise. Every part of it taught me something I didn't know before starting.
solution: The current private repo is being reshaped around the opportunity-hub workflow: student and employer auth, student profiles, company records, opportunity records, applications, and admin-curated opportunities before employer self-serve posting. The older marketplace screenshots remain here as engineering proof from the pre-pivot prototype.
callout: BayonHub is a real product pivot, not a rename. The public case study keeps the old prototype proof visible while the private repo moves toward the student opportunity platform.
tradeoffs: Kept the BayonHub brand while replacing the old classifieds domain model.
Started with admin-curated opportunities before employer self-serve posting so real students can apply sooner.
Kept the public source private because the pivot is a startup asset, while documenting the architecture and proof publicly.
whatIBuilt: A pre-pivot bilingual marketplace prototype with browsing, posting, search, account surfaces, and seller trust signals.
Offline/localStorage fallback patterns so public demos can run without API keys.
A pivot plan and private implementation direction for students, employers, opportunities, applications, and admin curation.
A product narrative that preserves honest proof from the old prototype while making the current opportunity-hub direction clear.
whatILearned: How to structure a React + Express product from scratch.
Why domain pivots require schema, copy, routes, and screenshots to move together.
How to think about trust in two-sided platforms.
Why phased, connected flows matter more than isolated flashy features.
How to preserve public proof while protecting startup source code.
How much harder building a real product is than following a tutorial.
repoNote: Source repository is private because BayonHub is an active startup asset. Public proof is provided through screenshots, case study notes, and validation history. The current product direction is the tech-student opportunity hub; older marketplace screenshots are retained only as proof from the pre-pivot prototype.
nextSteps: Finish the student experience loop: profile, browse opportunities, detail page, apply flow, and admin application visibility.
Add employer self-serve posting after the student loop can be tested with real users.
Replace the public case-study screenshots with opportunity-hub screenshots once the new surface is demo-ready.
